serving.description: 1 thosai with cheese, served with sambar and chutney
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one cheese thosai (approximately 250 g) served with sambar and coconut or tomato chutney at a mamak stall in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Thosai (Cheese) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) sourced directly from HPB Foodo at confidence 0.95. Micronutrients (calcium, iron, cholesterol, saturated fat, trans fat, vitamin C) were supplemented from HPB Foodo and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for cheese-filled dosa analogues. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, and remaining micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Dosa (fermented rice-lentil pancake) — GI analogous estimate based on plain dosa GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for cheese thosai found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is considered low according to the University of Sydney GI Database classification (GI <= 55 is low). This is estimated from plain dosa / fermented rice-lentil pancake entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Cheese filling adds protein and fat which may modestly lower the effective GI; the estimate of 55 is therefore considered conservative and defensible. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 36.81 × 2.5) / 100 ≈ 50.5 for a 250 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 844 mg per 100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit cheese thosai; sodium at 844 mg/100 g (2110 mg per 250 g serving) represents a very high single-meal sodium load — request reduced cheese and salt, and avoid additional salty accompaniments such as high-sodium sambar or chutneys.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 36.8 g/100 g with low GI (55) and modest dietary fibre (2.3 g/100 g); cheese protein provides partial glycaemic buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to half a thosai (approximately 125 g), pair with high-fibre sambar to slow glucose absorption, and monitor post-meal blood glucose, as the low-GI fermented rice-lentil base combined with a 250 g serving delivers approximately 92 g of carbohydrate with a glycaemic load of ~50.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 844 mg/100 g, saturated fat 4.8 g/100 g, trans fat 0.2 g/100 g, and cholesterol 80 mg/100 g collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume cheese thosai only occasionally and in small portions; the combination of very high sodium, moderate saturated fat, and measurable trans fat is unfavourable for blood pressure and lipid management — request reduced cheese and salt, and avoid ghee or butter-based accompaniments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 844 mg/100 g worsens tissue oedema and joint inflammation; tomato chutney accompaniment contains nightshade solanine
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid cheese thosai due to very high sodium content (844 mg/100 g) which exacerbates joint swelling; additionally, tomato chutney served as an accompaniment contains nightshade compounds that may aggravate symptoms in solanine-sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Thosai with Cheese (also written Cheese Thosai or Cheese Dosa) is a popular mamak-style variant of the classic South Indian fermented rice-and-lentil crepe, widely served at mamak stalls and Indian-Muslim eateries across Singapore and Malaysia. The batter is made from a fermented blend of rice flour and urad dal (black lentil), which is spread thinly on a hot griddle and cooked until crisp at the edges; processed cheese is melted onto the surface before folding or rolling. The result is a savoury, slightly tangy crepe with a crisp exterior, soft interior, and rich, salty cheese flavour. It is typically served with coconut chutney, tomato chutney, and sambar (a lentil-vegetable broth). Nutritionally, a 100 g portion provides 268 kcal, 9.7 g protein, 9.1 g fat (4.8 g saturated), 36.8 g carbohydrate, 2.3 g dietary fibre, and a notably high 844 mg sodium — primarily from the cheese and salted batter. The GI is estimated at 55 (low), placing it in a low glycaemic category, though a standard 250 g serving delivers a glycaemic load of approximately 50. Clinically, the very high sodium content makes this dish unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. The low GI and high carbohydrate load require portion control for people with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is vegetarian and halal-certified in most mamak establishments.
common_user_queries: How many calories in cheese thosai?
Is cheese thosai suitable for diabetics?
Can I eat cheese thosai if I have high blood pressure?
What is the sodium content of cheese thosai?
Is cheese dosa the same as cheese thosai?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore at confidence 0.95 for an exact food match (Thosai cheese). Micronutrients cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for cheese-filled dosa analogues. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database plain dosa entries with documented analogous basis.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, mineral panel, B-vitamin panel, and moisture data for cheese thosai are not separately reported in the HPB Foodo Singapore database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Calcium (100 mg), iron (1.0 mg), cholesterol (80 mg), saturated fat (4.8 g), trans fat (0.2 g), and vitamin C (2.0 mg) values from the input were sourced from HPB Foodo and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for cheese dosa analogues; grabfood was not used as a primary evidence source. Two default portions were present in the input (250 g and 300 g); 250 g is used as the canonical serving size as it is the more conservative and commonly cited mamak portion. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as (55 × 36.81 × 2.5) / 100 = 50.5 for a 250 g serving. No core macro fields are zero; zero-macro justification rule does not apply.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' as GI 55 falls within the 'low' category (GI <= 55) according to the University of Sydney GI Database. Updated relevant text in gi_source and health_context for Type 2 Diabetes to reflect 'low GI'.
Confirmed nutrient_source is compliant with approved sources (HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy). This addresses the G2 and Q2 failures from the previous iteration.
